headline: Is Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) Offering A Long Term Opportunity After Recent Share Price Weakness
summary: If you are wondering whether Berkshire Hathaway's current share price still reflects its underlying value, you are not alone. Many long term investors use it as a reference point for what a fairly priced blue chip looks like. The stock last closed at US$480.17, with returns of a 2.8% decline over 7 days, 0.1% decline over 30 days, 3.4% decline year to date, 5.9% decline over 1 year, 51.5% over 3 years and 86.4% over 5 years, which gives a mixed picture of recent momentum compared with longer...

headline: The Case for Buying the Dip as Mideast Conflict Widens
summary: As the conflict in the Middle East widened Monday following U.S. strikes on Iran, JPMorgan analyst Mislav Matejka published a note suggesting a similar approach.  Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group, notes data showing the median  gain three months after a major market shock, including everything from the attacks on Pearl Harbor in 1940 to last year’s tariff-induced chaos, is 2.7%.

content.title: The Case for Buying the Dip as Mideast Conflict Widens
content.summary: As the conflict in the Middle East widened Monday following U.S. strikes on Iran, JPMorgan analyst Mislav Matejka published a note suggesting a similar approach. headline: New Berkshire CEO Greg Abel calls Buffett a hard act to follow
summary: Glenview Trust Company chief investment officer Bill Stone joins Market Domination host Josh Lipton to discuss Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-B, BRK-A) CEO Greg Abel's first shareholder letter and what it might indicate about the company's future without Warren Buffett. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway Weighs Greg Abel Era Against Rising PacifiCorp Risks
summary: Greg Abel has taken over as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A), issuing his first shareholder letter that outlines his leadership approach and focus areas. S&P Global is considering a downgrade of PacifiCorp, a Berkshire subsidiary, in response to rising wildfire related legal and financial liabilities. headline: Berkshire CEO Abel Is Fond of Financial Metric Similar to One Criticized by Buffett
summary: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel is fond of a financial metric—net cash flow from operations—that is similar to a measure that former CEO Warren Buffett and vice chairman Charlie Munger long criticized.  In his inaugural annual shareholder letter released Saturday, Abel repeatedly referred to Berkshire’s net cash flow from operations, both for the overall company and for key divisions like the BNSF railroad,  Berkshire Hathaway  Energy (the company’s big utility), Precision Castparts and truck-stop operator Pilot.

content.title: Berkshire CEO Abel Is Fond of Financial Metric Similar to One Criticized by Buffett

headline: Berkshire Provides Scant Fourth-Quarter Financials. Investors Need to Do Their Own Calculations.
summary: One of the challenges of analyzing  Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter earnings is that it offers little specific information about the quarter in its financial reports.  Aside from its earnings press release, Berkshire doesn’t provide much financial data or commentary on the period.  There is no fourth-quarter income statement or cash-flow statement.

content.title: Berkshire Provides Scant Fourth-Quarter Financials. Investors Need to Do Their Own Calculations.
